snippet.title: How Safra Catz Made Oracle $100 Billion and Defied Larry Ellison's Advice
snippet.description:
In this captivating video, we delve into the untold story of Safra Catz, the brilliant mastermind behind Oracle's astonishing success in the tech industry. Join us as we uncover how she defied the advice of Oracle's founder, Larry Ellison, and single-handedly made the company $100 billion richer.

Oracle, a $300 billion software powerhouse, is famous for its enigmatic founder, Larry Ellison, who not only owns a luxurious Hawaiian island but also holds the title of the fourth richest person on earth. Yet, it is Safra Catz, the remarkable CEO of Oracle, who orchestrated the eminent dominance of this tech giant.

Ascending to the position of co-president within just five years of joining Oracle in 2004, Safra Catz exhibited her extraordinary negotiation skills by spearheading the acquisition of over 130 companies, some worth billions of dollars. However, when she became a co-CEO in 2014, Oracle faced a significant challenge: Larry Ellison's refusal to invest in cloud technology.

Against all odds, Safra Catz defied the skepticism of industry analysts and boldly led Oracle into the uncharted waters of the cloud market in 2016. Today, a staggering 70% of Oracle's annual revenue, which amounts to a colossal $50 billion, is generated from its triumphant foray into the cloud. Remarkably, this division continues to grow at an astonishing rate of 50% each year.

Since assuming the role of CEO, Safra Catz has not only transformed Oracle's fortunes but also boosted Larry Ellison's net worth by a mind-boggling $100 billion. Under her extraordinary leadership, Oracle's share price has skyrocketed with a staggering 200% increase.

Join us on this mesmerizing journey as we unveil the lesser-known tale of Safra Catz's relentless determination and strategic prowess, which propelled Oracle to unprecedented heights. Discover how her unwavering belief in the power of innovation and her unwavering commitment to defying conventional wisdom rewrote the rulebook in the tech industry.

snippet.title: Learn from the outliers: Safra Catz
snippet.description:
4 main bullets that explain negotiation skills:

Planning and preparation. Before entering a negotiation, it is important to do your research and understand the other party's position. You should also have a clear idea of your own goals and what you are willing to compromise on.

Active listening. One of the most important skills in negotiation is the ability to listen actively to the other party. This means paying attention to what they are saying, both verbally and nonverbally. It also means being able to ask clarifying questions and summarize their points back to them.

Communication. Effective communication is essential in any negotiation. This means being clear and concise in your own communication, as well as being able to understand the other party's communication. It is also important to be able to build rapport with the other party and create a positive atmosphere for negotiation.

Problem-solving. A good negotiator is able to find creative solutions that meet the needs of both parties. This means being able to think outside the box and come up with win-win solutions.

In addition to these four main skills, there are a number of other factors that can contribute to successful negotiation, such as confidence, flexibility, and the ability to manage emotions.

snippet.title: Leadership is more setting context than taking decisions, says Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser
snippet.description:
Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser is leading the global investment bank through its biggest transformation in more than two decades. She tells CNA’s Dawn Tan that leadership requires knowing when to get out of the way. She adds that it is more about setting the context rather than taking decisions, as those deeper within the organisation may have more information and data to make the call.

snippet.title: Elevate your career: An interview with Merran Kelsall
snippet.description:
Merran Kelsall, CPA Australia President and Chairman, has over 25 years’ experience on numerous boards and committees in the private and public sectors. 

She operated in public practice for 20 years, including 10 years as a partner at BDO. Her industry experience includes financial and professional services, education, insurance, and health.

As part of INTHEBLACK's December special edition, we interviewed Merran to uncover what it takes to pursue a career less ordinary.

snippet.title: How Jensen Huang builds teams that drive the world’s fastest AI
snippet.description:
NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang reveals the leadership philosophies and management practices that have made NVIDIA a powerhouse of AI and accelerated computing.

Jensen discusses why first-principles thinking is at the core of his decision-making, how he shapes company strategy through action—not words—and why he believes in empowering teams to pursue “barely possible” challenges. He shares his approach to building an organization that attracts exceptional talent, values deep domain expertise, and maintains a flat hierarchy where information travels quickly and everyone contributes.

Jensen also reflects on how leadership means creating an environment for others to thrive, why flat organizations win, and how empowering employees at every level leads to breakthrough innovation.

Whether you’re a founder, manager, or aspiring leader, this interview is packed with actionable wisdom and inspiration from one of technology’s most respected CEOs.

What's in this episode
- How Jensen challenged classic management playbooks to create a nimble, empowered organization
- The value of direct communication, transparency, and learning from everyone in the company
- Why NVIDIA avoids siloed structures, status reports, and commodity work
- The “top five things” system for keeping a pulse on the company and aligning action with strategy
- Why continuous, adaptive planning beats rigid five-year plans in fast-moving industries
- The importance of humility, adaptability, and changing your mind as a leader
- Insights on creating a culture where people can do their life’s best work—and walk away from the wrong work

About Jensen Huang and Joel Hellermark
Jensen Huang is the founder and CEO of NVIDIA, the world’s leading company in AI computing and graphics technology. Since founding NVIDIA in 1993, Huang has led the company from a startup to a global powerhouse, driving breakthroughs in deep learning, accelerated computing, and high-performance graphics. 

Joel Hellermark is the founder and CEO of Sana. An enterprising child, Joel taught himself to code in C at age 13 and founded his first company, a video recommendation technology, at 16. In 2021, Joel topped the Forbes 30 Under 30. Sana has been recognized on the Forbes AI 50 as one of the startups developing the most promising business use cases of artificial intelligence.

About Strange Loop
Strange Loop is a podcast about how artificial intelligence is reshaping the systems we live and work in. Each episode features deep, unscripted conversations with thinkers and builders reimagining intelligence, leadership, and the architectures of progress. The goal is not just to follow AI’s trajectory, but to question the assumptions guiding it.

Subscribe for more conversations at the edge of AI and human knowledge.

snippet.title: How Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang’s Big Bet on A I  is Powering ChatGPT and Changing the Future
snippet.description:
Nvidia dominates the market for graphics processing units (GPUs), which it entered in 1999 with the GeForce 256. Gaming brought in over $9 billion in revenue for Nvidia last year despite a recent downturn.But Nvidia’s latest earnings beat points to a new phenomenon in the GPU business. The technology is now at the center of the boom in artificial intelligence.

“We had the good wisdom to go put the whole company behind it,” CEO Jensen Huang told CNBC in an interview last month. “We saw early on, about a decade or so ago, that this way of doing software could change everything. And we changed the company from the bottom all the way to the top and sideways. Every chip that we made was focused on artificial intelligence.”

As the engine behind large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT, Nvidia is finally reaping rewards for its early investment in AI. That’s helped to cushion the blow from broader semiconductor industry struggles tied to U.S.-China trade tensions and a global chip shortage. 

Title Contents:

1: Popularizing the GPU
2: From graphics to AI and ChatGPT
3: Geopolitics and other concerns
4: Amazon, autonomous cars and beyond

snippet.title: Zaha Hadid: The Woman Who Changed the Face of Architecture
snippet.description:
This video tells the story of visionary architect, Zaha Hadid, who was born in Baghdad, Iraq in 1950. Hadid was exposed to the power of art and design from an early age, which ignited her passion for pushing the boundaries of what was possible in architecture. After studying mathematics at the American University of Beirut, Hadid moved to London to study at the Architectural Association School of Architecture where she immersed herself in the experimental architecture movement and worked for prestigious firms until she established her own firm, Zaha Hadid Architects, in 1979.

Despite facing discrimination as a Muslim woman in the male-dominated field of architecture, Hadid remained determined to break barriers and push the limits of what was possible. Hadid was known for her innovative furniture designs and was a pioneer in using computer software to create complex geometries and forms. She was also a professor of architecture and an accomplished artist. Her legacy includes her striking buildings, such as the Guangzhou Opera House in Guangzhou, China, and the Heydar Aliyev Center in Baku, Azerbaijan.

Tragically, Hadid passed away in 2016, but her impact on the field of architecture cannot be overstated. Throughout her career, she received numerous awards and honors, including being the first woman to receive the prestigious Pritzker Architecture Prize in 2004. Despite her untimely passing, Hadid's legacy continues to inspire architects and designers all around the globe, paving the way for future generations to follow in her footsteps.

snippet.title: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang weighs in the Fed hiking interest rates
snippet.description:
#gaming #youtube #stockmarket 
Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell raised interest rates by another quarter point Wednesday, against the backdrop of chaos in the banking sector. In a wide ranging interview, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang told Yahoo Finance's Julie Hyman and Dan Howley how the change in rates would affect the chipmaking business. "This is the time when we all have to do more with less," Jensen said, "and accelerated computing is really the best path forward to do so."

snippet.title: Nvidia CEO on AI Revolution #shorts
snippet.description:
This video shows the #nvidia GTC 2023 event with CEO Jensen Huang. The video shows the intro event about NVIDIA's new AI strategy including #chatgpt and other #ai features.

GTC stands for GPU Technology Conference. NVIDIA’s GTC is an annual event that focuses on breakthroughs in AI, accelerated computing, and beyond. The conference is for the era of AI and the metaverse. The GTC 2023 event will take place from March 20-23, 2023. The event will feature over 650 expert-led sessions, including 70 alone on generative AI. The event will also have a keynote by NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, who will share the latest breakthroughs in generative AI, cloud services, industrial digitalization, and more.

Jensen Huang is a Taiwanese American billionaire business magnate, electrical engineer, and the co-founder, current president, and CEO of Nvidia Corporation. He was born on February 17, 1963, in Tainan, Taiwan. His family emigrated to the United States when he was 9 years old. Huang received his undergraduate degree in electrical engineering from Oregon State University in 1984 and his master’s degree in electrical engineering from Stanford University in 1992. Prior to founding NVIDIA, Mr. Huang held a variety of positions from 1985 to 1993 at LSI Logic Corp., a computer chip manufacturer, and from 1984 to 1985 at Advanced Micro Devices, Inc., a semiconductor company.

Thanks for watching the video and we hope you love it!

snippet.title: Bob Iger Says He Prevented Marvel Chair Isaac Perlmutter From Firing
snippet.description:
Disney CEO Bob Iger on Thursday called Marvel chairman Isaac Perlmutter’s backing of activist investor Nelson Peltz “a curious dynamic. ”Peltz, of Trian Group, this morning abandoned his battle with Disney that featured a push for a seat on the board. He said a wide-ranging corporate restructuring announced Wednesday spoke to many of his concerns about the company’s management and strategy. Peltz’s campaign didn’t go public until mid-last year, but it started back in 2022 under former CEO Bob Chapek and with Perlmutter’s support, Disney has said. In an SEC filing in January as part of its proxy fight with Peltz, Disney lists meetings and calls starting in the summer of 2022 involving Peltz and Chapek, CFO Christine McCarthy, directors Amy Chang and Safra Catz, General Counsel Horatio Gutierrez, as well as Iger. A number of them were initiated by Perlmutter, who supported Peltz and lobbied Disney execs and board members on his behalf.“Peltz, or Isaac Perlmutter (an employee and shareholder of Disney who currently serves as chairman of Marvel Entertainment) on Peltz’ behalf, asked for a Board seat or suggested he should be added to the Board no less than 20 times since July 2022, ” Disney said in the filing. RELATED: Disney Earnings Report & Reorganization – Full CoverageIn an interview with CNBC, Iger indicated that Peltz wasn’t happy at losing oversight of Marvel’s movie-making operations.“Our filings indicate that both Ike and Nelson were working together to try to encourage the board or convince the board to put Nelson on the board, ” Iger said. “They have a relationship that dates back quite some time. We bought Marvel in 2009. I promised Ike the job that he would continue to run Marvel after that. Not forever, necessarily. But after that. And in 2015 he was intent on firing Kevin Feige who was running Marvel’s studio, the movie-making [operation] at the time, and I thought that was a mistake and stepped in to prevent that from happening. I think Kevin is an incredibly, incredibly talented executive that you know, the Marvel track record speaks for itself. And so I moved the movie-making operation of Marvel out from under Ike into the movie studio under Alan Horn” (with Feige reporting directly to Horn, who retired from the company in 2021). Asked by host David Faber if that created ill will, Iger said, “You’d have to ask Ike about that. But let’s put it this way. He was not happy about it. And I think that unhappiness exists today. And you know, what the link is between that and Nelson, his relationship. I think that’s something that you can speculate about. I won’t. ”


All data is taken from the source: http://deadline.com
Article Link: https://deadline.com/2023/02/bob-iger-says-prevented-marvel-chair-isaac-perlmutter-from-firing-kevin-feige-in-2015-1235254540/

For Karen Lynch, being recognized as one of the most powerful women in America was just the beginning of her journey to becoming an even more powerful woman. Since those early days, she has experienced many successes, but also faced plenty of challenges and disappointments. Here’s how Karen Lynch was able to turn her vision into reality by applying her own five principles on how to be a powerful woman.

Karen’s Story
Karen was born in Santa Monica, California. Her father’s death when she was only nine years old affected her tremendously. She moved with her mother and siblings to Whittier where they settled in a low-income neighborhood. They lived in government housing and Karen remembers how difficult it was on her mom; she took care of Karen and four other children, working two jobs most of her life. Even with all their challenges, Karen’s mother set an example for her children that showed them if you persevere and work hard you can be successful no matter what your circumstances are...

A Day in the Life of Karen Lynch
I wake up each morning and enjoy a cup of coffee on my terrace. My mornings are so peaceful, that is until I try to get our four-year-old son out of bed for school. He loves to stay in bed as long as possible which gives me time to read or write. For breakfast, I usually have oatmeal with blueberries and strawberries or protein pancakes with blackberries and whipped cream, paired with milk or my husband’s french press coffee, depending on how much time I have before work. When Alex goes off to daycare (he’s currently at home full-time), my daughter and I head out for her morning classes in Midtown East.Tips from Karen on How to Stand Out as an Influencer in the Digital World
Own Your Power! I don’t feel like it’s about more experience or being older. It’s about your point of view, it’s about you as an individual owning your power and saying what I have to say matters. Do we ever really listen when someone is just telling us facts? Maybe in school. But in life, people want experience and they want genuine—and that comes from how you deliver it and how authentic you are. If people can see through something or if they know that what I am saying is not coming from my heart, then I think they will lose respect for me no matter what my resume looks like.

How to Become a Powerful Woman Like Karen
Karen was only one of four women in her senior year of high school. She had goals and dreams, but they were considered pretty far-fetched by all of her peers. She graduated and went on to college; while she didn’t have big ambitions at first, she was majoring in business management because it just made sense. On top of that, she worked full-time for some extra cash, so she was able to get an apartment with three other girls after graduation. It wasn’t long before Karen realized that she didn’t like where things were going—she wanted more from life than just a 9-to-5 desk job or getting married and having kids.

Lessons Learned from Karen on Anxiety While Traveling

snippet.description:

        " Top 10 Highest-Paid Ceos " :

Who are top ceo in the world ? 

Table of Contents

  1.  Elon Musk ( $23.5 billion )
  2. TIM COOK ( $770.5 million )
  3. JENSEN HUANG ( $561 million )
  4. REED HASTINGS ( $453.5 million )
  5. LEONARD SCHLEIFER ( $452.9 million )

  8. ROBERT A. KOTICK ( $296.7 million )
  9. HOCK E. TAN ( $288 million )
  10. SAFRA A. CATZ (  $239.5 million )

ROLE OF A CEO?

Being one of the most important figures of a particular organization, the roles, and work of a CEO varies from one company to another. Usually, it depends on the organizational structure or sometimes the size of the company.

For instance, in smaller companies, the CEO takes on more “involving roles” e.g. hiring a staff. Whereas in larger companies, the CEOs usually deal with upper-tier corporate strategies and major decision-making tasks.

An efficient CEO is necessary for the company to grow and achieve great success. It is the CEO of the company who makes crucial decisions. In this article, we will talk about the world’s highest-paid CEOs and how their skills and decision making strategies make them world-class leaders.

snippet.title: BP CEO resigns over relationships with colleagues
snippet.description:
BP (BP) shares closed the day lower as CEO Bernard Looney resigned after acknowledging he had not been "fully transparent" about personal relationships he had with colleagues, according to a statement from the company. The statement says an investigation is ongoing and that relationships occurred before Looney became CEO. BP CFO Murray Auchincloss will act as interim CEO. Yahoo Finance Live breaks down the story.
